name: La Caduerna MTB Trail
name_original: La Caduerna — ruta transfronteriza con Portugal desde Nuez de Aliste (IMBA)
description: Explore the moderate La Caduerna mountain biking route, a 23.8-mile transboundary trail between Nuez de Aliste, Spain, and Portugal.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Trabazos traverse diverse landscapes, including the Montesinho Natural Park. The region is characterized by moderate slopes and enclosed valleys carved by the Manzanas River, with abrupt banks contributing to a distinct microclimate. This terrain offers varied conditions for mountain biking, from challenging ascents to scenic paths through natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Trabazos The most popular mountain bike trail is La Raya — ruta circular desde Trabazos, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Caduerna MTB Trail, a moderate 23.8 miles (38.2 km) path. This route offers a balanced challenge with diverse scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Oil Route Trail, a 18.1 miles (29.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Trabazos is defined by moderate slopes, river valleys, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult terrain. The routes in Trabazos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Trabazos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain bike trails in Trabazos?
A: Trabazos offers a range of mountain bike trails, primarily categorized as moderate and difficult. You'll find 7 moderate routes and 3 difficult routes, providing options for various skill levels. The terrain often includes moderate slopes and varied landscapes within the Montesinho Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Trabazos?
A: Yes, several circular mountain bike routes are available. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018426876/la-raya-ruta-circular-desde-trabazos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Raya — ruta circular desde Trabazos&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 29.9-mile (48.1 km) trail. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1260473611/mahide-desde-figueruela-de-arriba-circular-por-el-campo-de-aliste" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mahide desde Figueruela de Arriba — circular por el Campo de Aliste&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 20.3-mile (32.7 km) route.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while mountain biking in Trabazos?
A: The region around Trabazos is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You might encounter the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña el Pingón Cave and Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Canzona&lt;/a&gt;, known for its restored water retention dams. The trails also offer views within the Montesinho Natural Park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1956537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of São Julião&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area offers cultural insights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuez de Aliste Historic Village Center&lt;/a&gt; is a picturesque village with traditional Alistana architecture. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Beneita Petroglyphs&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into ancient history.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Trabazos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Trabazos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars. Riders often appreciate the diverse landscapes, from moderate slopes to enclosed valleys carved by the Manzanas River, and the challenging yet scenic routes available.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in Trabazos?
A: The duration varies depending on the trail's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018775114/la-caduerna-mtb-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caduerna MTB Trail&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 23.8 miles (38.2 km), typically takes around 3 hours and 56 minutes. A shorter, moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1259962640/oil-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oil Route Trail&lt;/a&gt; (18.1 miles / 29.2 km) can be completed in about 2 hours and 57 minutes.

Q: Is Trabazos suitable for advanced mountain bikers looking for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Trabazos offers several challenging routes for advanced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018426876/la-raya-ruta-circular-desde-trabazos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Raya — ruta circular desde Trabazos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29.9-mile (48.1 km) trail with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding experience through varied terrain.

Q: What is the general terrain like for mountain biking in Trabazos?
A: The terrain in Trabazos is diverse, characterized by moderate slopes and enclosed valleys. You'll ride through areas carved by the Manzanas River, with abrupt banks contributing to a distinct microclimate. The routes often traverse varied landscapes within the Montesinho Natural Park, offering a mix of ascents and scenic paths.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Trabazos?
A: Yes, you can find scenic viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1956537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of São Julião in the Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive vistas, allowing you to appreciate the region's natural beauty.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Trabazos?
A: There are at least 10 mountain bike trails around Trabazos listed in this guide, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Trabazos?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's diverse landscapes and natural park setting suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for mountain biking. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

Q: Is it possible to find a mountain bike route that passes through a traditional village?
A: Yes, some routes or nearby areas offer this experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuez de Aliste Historic Village Center&lt;/a&gt; is a well-preserved traditional village with unique architecture, and while not directly on a listed route, it's a notable point of interest in the vicinity that riders might explore.
tips: We are in the picturesque town of Nuez de Aliste with its particular architecture embodied in its streets. The remarkable conservation of numerous buildings built in the traditional style of Alistana architecture stands out. It is also worth visiting the Church of the Virgin of the Assumption.
Nice bridge that crosses the Arroyo del Valle. Nearby we will find a recreational area in case we want to take a break and observe the small waterfalls that form in the place.

A beautiful stretch in which we cross the Arroyo de la Riberica through the gate and from where the Fillival mill is just 200 meters away, one of the many flour mills in the region that gives us a beautiful picture.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The La Caduerna route starts from Nuez de Aliste, a historic village in Spain. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a registered route, it's highly likely there are designated parking areas in or near the village center, which serves as the starting point for many local routes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, primarily consisting of cart tracks with steep inclines, forest tracks, and singletrack paths. Only a small section of 1.9 km is on paved road. You'll ride through 'altonazos' (high, flat areas), winding paths with lush vegetation, and cross various riverbanks and springs. This mix suggests a dynamic riding experience with both climbs and descents.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: The route is officially rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. The presence of 'pendientes pronunciadas' (steep inclines) on cart tracks suggests that some sections could be challenging. While not explicitly advanced, it requires good fitness and riding skills, so it might be demanding for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What natural and cultural sights can I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter picturesque views with abundant chestnut, oak, and reforested pine trees. The route passes through the influence area of the LIC Riberas del río Manzanas y Afluentes, a protected natural space. Notable cultural sights in Nuez de Aliste include the Fuente Grande (or Roman Fountain) and the Ricasenda fountain, as well as traditional Alistan architecture like the Santa María Church. Along the route, you can also see remnants of historical flour mills, such as Molino de Lujo, and recreational areas like La Chanera on the Manzanas River, which borders Montesinho Natural Park in Portugal. Don't miss the Nuez de Aliste Historic Village Center, Puente de la Plaza, and The Pontón and the Fillival Mill.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. You might spot diverse wildlife, including the Iberian wolf, roe deer, deer, and wild boar. Along the Manzanas River, there's also a possibility of seeing otters.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and lush vegetation, spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant riding conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer could be enjoyable, especially near the fluvial beach, but consider potential heat. Winters might be challenging due to weather conditions on steep inclines and forest tracks.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas with abundant wildlife, specific rules regarding dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially in areas where Iberian wolves are present.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: The La Caduerna route is officially registered with the Royal Spanish Cycling Federation's National Register of Mountain Bike Routes. This typically means it's a publicly accessible trail and does not require a specific permit or entrance fee for use.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 38.2 kilometers (23.7 miles) long and typically takes around 3 hours and 49 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of a larger network. It is one of three routes converging at a BTT (Mountain Bike) center in Trabazos. Additionally, small sections of the route overlap with the Camino Portugués de la vía de la Plata (1%), Mtb Camino Portugués de la vía de la Plata (1%), and Calle Colaguita (1%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Nuez de Aliste is a historic village, and as such, it's reasonable to expect local amenities like cafes or pubs. The presence of a fluvial beach popular in summer and the village's cultural significance suggest there are likely options for refreshments and possibly accommodation in or very close to Nuez de Aliste.
